name: Coconut Prawn Curry
tagline: Quick prawn curry in coconut gravy — zinc, copper, selenium, B12
highlight: Add prawns last and cook just 4 minutes — they go rubbery in seconds past that

hero.alt: Pink prawns in a creamy yellow coconut curry with curry leaves
hero.caption: prawn curry — coconut, curry leaf, light

healthFacts: 100g prawns: 1.6mg zinc, 0.4mg copper, 38µg selenium — trace minerals fully covered.
Vitamin B12 content (1.5µg/100g) supports nerves and red blood cells.
Iodine from shellfish supports thyroid hormone synthesis (TSH-relevant).
Astaxanthin (the pink pigment) is a potent antioxidant; survives gentle cooking.

steps.title: Marinate the prawns
steps.detail: Toss prawns with ¼ tsp turmeric, ¼ tsp salt, ¼ tsp Kashmiri chili. Set aside while you make the gravy.
steps.tip: Pre-seasoned prawns hold flavour better than seasoning the gravy alone.

steps.title: Build the gravy
steps.detail: Heat coconut oil. Crackle mustard seeds, add curry leaves, then onion. Cook till golden. Add ginger-garlic, then tomato, remaining turmeric and chili. Cook 5 minutes till tomato breaks down.
steps.tip: Cook the masala dry first — adding coconut milk to under-cooked masala = flat curry.

steps.title: Add coconut milk and prawns
steps.detail: Pour in coconut milk, bring to a gentle simmer. Slip in the prawns and cook on low for 3–4 minutes till they just turn pink and curl. Pull off the heat. Serve with steamed rice.
steps.tip: Don't boil coconut milk — it splits. Keep it on the lowest simmer.
